cases.leeway: extra freedom or room to make decisions without strict limits
cases.rescind: to officially cancel or take back a decision or law
cases.unperturbed: calm and undisturbed despite something that would normally upset someone
cases.pompous: self-important and arrogant, especially in speech or manner
cases.repercussion: an indirect consequence of an action, felt later
cases.capricious: prone to sudden unpredictable changes in mood or behavior
cases.complacency: smug self-satisfaction that makes someone unaware of problems
cases.ostracize: to deliberately exclude someone from a group
cases.nondescript: lacking any distinctive or memorable features; plain
cases.anathema: something a person detests utterly and would never accept
cases.stygian: extremely dark and gloomy, like something out of the underworld
cases.extraneous: not relevant or necessary to the main matter at hand
cases.unsightly: unpleasant or ugly to look at
cases.unfathomable: impossible to fully understand or grasp
cases.ingenuity: cleverness and inventiveness in solving a problem
cases.knick-knack: a small decorative trinket with little practical value
cases.belligerent: hostile and eager to fight or argue
cases.procure: to obtain something, especially through deliberate effort
cases.unparalleled: having no equal; unmatched by anything else
cases.fieldfare: a type of migratory thrush bird found across Europe
cases.flatulence: intestinal gas, or the act of releasing it
cases.marionette: a puppet controlled from above by strings
cases.archduke: a European title of nobility ranking above an ordinary duke
cases.stooge: someone manipulated or used by another person to do their bidding
cases.disparage: to speak of someone or something in a belittling, dismissive way
cases.jinx: to bring bad luck by mentioning something prematurely
cases.hedonistic: focused on pursuing pleasure and self-indulgence above all else
cases.cerebral: relying on careful reasoning and intellect rather than emotion
cases.luminary: a person who is an inspiring, leading figure in their field
cases.promiscuous: having casual sexual relationships with many different partners
cases.amiable: friendly, pleasant, and easy to get along with
cases.debauchery: excessive indulgence in sensual pleasures and vice
cases.braggart: someone who boasts constantly about their own achievements
cases.schism: a deep division or split between opposing factions
cases.unequivocally: in a way that leaves absolutely no doubt or ambiguity
cases.sleuth: a detective or someone skilled at investigating mysteries
cases.requited: of love or feelings, returned by the other person
cases.ruminate: to think deeply and repeatedly about something
cases.schadenfreude: pleasure taken from another person's misfortune
cases.swindle: to cheat someone out of money through deception
cases.foray: a brief attempt or first venture into a new activity
cases.seedy: sordid, run-down, and disreputable in appearance
cases.melancholic: feeling a deep, persistent sadness or gloominess
cases.pipsqueak: an insignificant or unimportant person, used dismissively
cases.unbeknownst: without someone's knowledge; unknown to them
cases.rebut: to argue against and try to disprove a claim
cases.wishy-washy: vague, indecisive, and lacking firm conviction
cases.philanthropy: the practice of donating money or effort to help others
cases.larceny: the crime of stealing someone else's property
cases.heed: careful attention paid to advice or a warning
cases.abhorrence: a strong feeling of hatred or disgust toward something
cases.whippersnapper: a young, inexperienced person seen as overconfident
cases.moratorium: an official temporary halt or ban on an activity
cases.loath: reluctant or unwilling to do something
cases.failsafe: designed so it cannot fail even if something goes wrong
cases.loophole: a gap or ambiguity in rules that lets someone avoid them
cases.turn over a new leaf: start behaving better after a period of bad conduct
cases.the die is cast: a decision has been made and can no longer be undone
cases.wiggle out of: to cleverly avoid a responsibility or obligation
cases.take the plunge: to finally commit to something risky after hesitating
cases.broach the subject: to bring up a sensitive or difficult topic for the first time
cases.bargaining chip: something used as leverage to gain an advantage in a negotiation
cases.chew it over: to think about something carefully before deciding
cases.go weak at the knees: to feel suddenly overwhelmed, often by attraction or fear
cases.put on airs: to act as though one is more important or refined than they are
cases.quick on the uptake: fast at understanding or catching on to something new
cases.stop-gap measure: a temporary fix used until a permanent solution is found
cases.wet blanket: someone who spoils other people's fun or enthusiasm
cases.raison d'etre: the main reason something or someone exists
cases.faux pas: an embarrassing social mistake or blunder
cases.extenuating circumstances: factors that make a wrongdoing seem more excusable
cases.put the cart before the horse: to do things in the wrong order, reversing normal priorities
cases.pang of conscience: a sudden sharp feeling of guilt
cases.war of attrition: a drawn-out conflict where each side tries to wear the other down
cases.silver tongue: an eloquent, persuasive way of speaking
cases.silver screen: the movie industry or cinema in general
cases.draw straws: select someone at random from a group using sticks of different lengths
cases.rug pulled out from under one's feet: having something relied upon suddenly taken away
cases.strong-arm: to pressure or coerce someone using force or intimidation
cases.cream of the crop: the best members or examples of a group
